questions.guidance: or SC1 for $c = -39$ and $k = 3$. If correct in working e.g. written next to sequence and reversed on answer line then allow 2 marks with a TE

parts.label: (b)
parts.guidance: Tolerance within half a small square i.e. must be between 32 and 34 lines not on them

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.answer: Correct ruled line of best fit
subparts.guidance: Use template. Pass through (30, 8) to (30, 24) and (72, 50) to (72, 66). Condone good freehand

subparts.label: (ii)

markAllocation.criterion: FT their line of best fit provided line shows positive correlation and answer is an integer

subparts.guidance: Tolerance $\pm$ half a square from their value at 45 people

parts.label: (d)
parts.guidance: Allow +ve but not +. Ignore embellishments e.g. strong

markAllocation.criterion: for $\frac{1}{2} \times 6 \times (x + 9.5) = 42$ oe or better or for $42 \times \frac{2}{6} - 9.5$ oe

questions.guidance: Allow $\frac{9}{2}$ for 2 marks. Answer $\frac{27}{6}$ scores max M1. Method can be done in stages without referring to x

markAllocation.criterion: for $\frac{2}{7} \times \frac{11}{6}$ or $\frac{22}{77} \div \frac{42}{77}$ oe with common denominator

questions.guidance: OR M1 for $132 - 2x + 15 + 5x = 180$ oe. M1 for collecting x terms on one side and number terms on the other. M1 for $15 + 5 \times their\ x$ oe where $-3 < their\ x < 15$ or for $132 - 2 \times their\ x$ oe where $21 < their\ x < 66$

markAllocation.criterion: for answer 113 or 113.0 to 113.1... or an answer in terms of $\pi$ which rounds to 113

questions.guidance: or M1 for correct first step for finding $d$ or $r$. e.g. $72 + 72 = d^2$ oe or $72 = r^2 + r^2$ oe or $\sqrt{72} = \frac{r}{\sin 45}$ oe or $\frac{1}{2} \times r \times r = \frac{72}{4}$ oe. Ignore inaccurate values for 6 or 12 if recovered by accurate 36

parts.label: (a)

markAllocation.criterion: for $3(8x^2 - 3xy)$ or $x(24x - 9y)$ or $3x(8x - 3y)$ seen then spoilt

parts.guidance: Condone omission of final bracket for 1 or 2 marks

parts.label: (b)

markAllocation.criterion: for $(21x + 14y)(3x - 2y)$ or $(3x + 2y)(21x - 14y)$ or $7(3x + 2y)(3x - 2y)$ seen then spoilt

parts.guidance: or M1 for $7(9x^2 - 4y^2)$ or $[...](3x + 2y)(3x - 2y)$. Condone omission of final bracket for all marks

questions.guidance: Allow $\frac{28}{5}$ for 3 marks. e.g. $10.5 = k\sqrt{8 + 1}$, implied by $k = 3.5$. Method may be in one step e.g. $\frac{10.5}{\sqrt{8+1}} = \frac{y}{\sqrt{1.56+1}}$ oe

markAllocation.criterion: for correct region identified satisfying the given inequalities

questions.guidance: Penalise first occurrence of dashed/solid error. Lines can be any length and condone good freehand. Mark R first, if no R mark unshaded region

parts.label: (b)

markAllocation.criterion: for correct complete area statement e.g. $\frac{1}{2} \times 20 \times 10 + 7 \times 20$ oe

parts.guidance: or M1 for one correct area. Implied by 100 or 140. e.g. M1 for $20 \times 17 - k$ or $340 - k$ where $k \neq 0$

questions.guidance: Answers 60800, 60830 or 60840 without 60835 in the working score M1 only. For M1 isw if 40 000 subtracted. $40\,000 \times (1 + 15\%)^3$ is not sufficient for M1
Allow any equivalent e.g. $\frac{51}{60}$, 0.85, 85%. Not ratio 17:20

markAllocation.criterion: for $360 - 90 - 90 - 128$ oe or B1 for [obtuse angle] $AOC = 128$ or $AOD$ or $COD = 64$ or $DAO$ or $DCO = 90$

questions.guidance: Angles may be marked on diagram. Allow right angle symbol between radius and tangent on either side

parts.label: (a)
parts.answer: Correct Venn diagram with $\sqrt{0.08}$, $8 \times 10^{-1}$, $0.\overline{8}$ placed correctly
parts.guidance: or B1 for two correctly placed or correct conversion of $8 \times 10^{-1}$, 8% and $\sqrt{0.08}$ to 0.8, 0.08, 0.2[8...] or 0.3. Allow equivalent values in the diagram

parts.label: (b)
parts.answer: Correct Venn diagram shading for $(A \cup B) \cap C'$ or similar

parts.label: (a)
parts.guidance: or M1 for $[\frac{1}{2} \times] \frac{4}{3} \times \pi \times 4.3^3$ or $\pi \times 4.3^2 \times 11.9$ oe. Award 3 marks for answers $273.03\pi$ to $273.04\pi$ but M2 only for $273\pi$. For M marks, allow multiples of $\pi$

parts.label: (b)
parts.guidance: OR M1 for $\pi \times 4.3^2 \times k$ where $k$ is a whole number. M1 for $2 \times \pi \times 4.3 \times 11.9$. Award 4 marks for $157.8[1]\pi$ or $\frac{15781}{100}\pi$. Award M3 only for answer $158\pi$

markAllocation.criterion: for recognition of terms being powers of 7

questions.guidance: Allow unsimplified for 2 marks e.g. $\frac{7^n}{49}$, $\frac{1}{7} \times 7^{n-1}$, $(\frac{1}{7})^{2-n}$. e.g. any power of 7 shown e.g. $7^2$, or $n$th term $7^{an+b}$ or a correct answer seen then spoilt

markAllocation.criterion: for correct expansion of the three brackets unsimplified or for simplified four-term expression of correct form with three terms correct

questions.guidance: or B1 for correct expansion of two of the given brackets with at least three terms out of four correct. e.g. $x^2 + 3x + 5x + 15$, $8x$ is two terms correct. $2x^2 + 10x + x + 5$, $11x$ is two terms correct

markAllocation.criterion: for substitution of their midpoint into their $y = mx + c$ oe

questions.guidance: Allow equivalent final answers e.g. $y = -0.2x + 28$, $[y =] -\frac{6}{30}x + 28$. M1 implied by $[y =] 5x + k$, $m = 5$. M1M1 implied by $[y =] -\frac{1}{5}x + k$ oe. Do not allow their gradient of AB or substitution of A or B.

Allow $\frac{21}{5}$ for 3 marks. M2 implied by $\sqrt[3]{74.088}$. or M1 for $\frac{\sqrt[3]{172.8}}{\sqrt[3]{100}}$ oe or $\frac{\sqrt[3]{100}}{\sqrt[3]{172.8}}$ oe or $\frac{x^3}{3.5^3} = \frac{172.8}{100}$ oe
